adds.rationale: Robotics is named as a 'next wave' in the profile but the watchlist only carries ISRG (medical surgical robotics); Q2-25 saw the broader humanoid/industrial robotics theme inflect with Figure AI's $40B-valuation round and Tesla's Optimus production announcements, and ROBO is the cleanest US-listed pure-play robotics ETF to fill that thematic gap.

news_evidence.summary: Figure AI raised funding at a reported ~$40B valuation in February 2025, reflecting accelerating venture capital commitment to humanoid robotics as a near-term commercial wave.

ROBO Global Robotics & Automation Index ETF NPORT filing (Jan 31, 2025) confirms diversified exposure to industrial automation, medical robotics, and humanoid-adjacent suppliers (Serve Robotics, Globus Medical, Renishaw, Azenta).

Meta-Constellation 20-year 1.1 GW nuclear PPA (June 3, 2025) underscores that the hyperscaler-energy-AI-robotics build-out the profile bets on is moving from announcement to multi-decade contract, broadly supportive of automation capex.

removes.rationale: Apple SVP Eddy Cue's May 2025 antitrust testimony disclosed that Google search volume in Safari declined for the first time, attributed to users shifting to AI alternatives; combined with the ongoing DOJ remedies phase (potential Chrome/search-distribution remedies), the original 'Google search moat' thesis is showing genuine erosion, while AI exposure is preserved via MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, TSM, and PLTR.

news_evidence.summary: Apple SVP Eddy Cue testified in the DOJ Google search antitrust remedies trial that Safari Google searches fell for the first time in April 2025, attributing the decline to users moving to AI search tools; Alphabet shares dropped ~7% on the day.

DOJ remedies phase in US v. Google search-monopoly case proceeded through Q2-25, with proposed remedies including forced Chrome divestiture and restrictions on default-search payments to Apple - direct risk to Alphabet's traffic-acquisition-cost economics.

rationale_overall: Net watchlist stays at the cap of 16. One swap: add ROBO to fill the under-represented robotics wave the profile explicitly names (the watchlist previously had only ISRG, medical-surgical, with no exposure to the humanoid/industrial-automation inflection), and remove GOOGL on a genuine thesis-erosion signal from the May 2025 Apple-Cue testimony plus the active DOJ remedies phase. AI exposure remains robust (MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, TSM, PLTR); nuclear (CCJ, CEG, SMR, VRT), rockets (RKLB), quantum (IONQ), and geopolitical-energy (LNG, ITA) buckets are unchanged on intact theses, with the June 3 Meta-Constellation 20-year PPA further validating the hyperscaler-nuclear leg.
